evidence.summary: Development desktop build workflow fans out platform matrix builds, disables updater artifacts, and uploads CI bundles as artifacts.
Docs deployment workflow builds VitePress with npm on Node 22 and publishes Pages artifacts.
PR quality workflow routes checks by changed area and runs desktop, server, native, docs, and coverage gates.
PR triage workflow classifies changed paths into area labels and test expectations, including desktop native and docs surfaces.
Desktop release workflow validates version/release-notes alignment and builds tagged Tauri releases via GitHub Actions.
Desktop package declares the web build/test/lint/Tauri scripts and desktop app version.
Cargo lockfile records resolved native desktop dependency versions for the Tauri release surface.
Tauri Rust package pins runtime/build crates and updater, shell, dialog, process, notification, and single-instance plugins.
macOS plist carries notification-related bundle metadata for the desktop application.
Vector icon asset participates in desktop packaging and release branding surfaces.

Vector icon asset participates in desktop packaging and release branding surfaces.
Rust build script delegates native build-time configuration to tauri_build.
Default Tauri capability manifest grants core event, window, webview, app, and resources access for the desktop shell.

provisional_nodes.title: Agent, Channel, and Desktop Documentation Corpus

attrs.rationale: GitHub release workflows read Tauri version/config and build package bundles.
Tauri config plus icon set define packaged desktop assets and bundle identity.
Docs workflow builds docs/ via VitePress config and deploys to GitHub Pages.
Desktop architecture and installation docs explicitly describe desktop/src-tauri and build outputs.

observations.summary: The desktop release lane ties tag/app version to a matching release-notes filename before publishing.
The docs publishing surface uses npm ci and Node 22, which is operationally distinct from Bun-based project workflows.
The native desktop runtime bundles updater plugin support and explicit sidecar/process lifecycle management inside the Tauri library.
The assigned docs corpus includes bilingual agent content, but only the English agent subsection was in this packet scope, not English desktop/channel docs.
